title: Use inventory crate for open-world backend dispatch in from_uri
description: The from_uri and from_signing_uri functions in secretx/src/lib.rs are a hardcoded closed-world match over backend name strings, gated by #[cfg(feature)] guards. Adding a new backend requires editing that dispatch file. The inventory crate solves this with a linker-section trick: each backend crate calls inventory::submit! to register itself, and from_uri iterates the registry at runtime. This makes dispatch genuinely open-world — third-party crates can add backends without patching secretx. Daniel Franke suggested this in review and noted he contributed WASM support to inventory last year, removing the last major platform holdout. Work involves: adding BackendRegistration and SigningBackendRegistration types to secretx-core, adding inventory::submit! calls to each backend, replacing the match in from_uri with an iterator, and removing all #[cfg(feature)] guards from the dispatch file.
